Question: Changes to copyright law in the future are likely to focus on which issues? A. Reducing or eliminating DRM. B. Adjusting to new advances in digital technology. C. Enabling and regulating a secondary market for digital content. D. Shortening copyright term. E. All of the above.

• keeping the law in step with rapidly evolving digital technologies (cloud computing, streaming platforms, generative-AI systems, etc.); and  
• creating clear rules for a legitimate “second-hand” or resale market for purely digital works (the digital equivalent of used books, CDs, DVDs, games).

Proposals aimed at abolishing DRM altogether or at shortening the basic copyright term have very little political traction today, so they are not considered likely focal points.

Therefore, the best answers are:

B. Adjusting to new advances in digital technology, and  
C. Enabling and regulating a secondary market for digital content.

• Updating rights to keep pace with new digital‐tech developments  
• Creating a regulated secondary market for licensed digital works  
• Debating whether to shorten the now very long copyright term
